payload.text: Cậu ơi khi mà nhấn nút dừng đấy thì sẽ là lệnh tuyệt đối nha, như kiểu dù có chạm vào đâu hay lướt thì nó cũng sẽ không tự phát nhạc nua

**Considering Absolute Stops**

I'm currently focused on how to make the "Stop/Pause" button trigger an absolute stop in the music player. My thinking is to ensure that all user interactions cease music playback. I am researching ways to achieve this, even if the user interacts with the app interface after pressing the button. I want it to be a truly definitive stop.


**Prioritizing Manual Pauses**

I've made progress in preventing automatic music playback after a manual pause. I'm adding a `hasManuallyPaused` state variable. The "unlock audio" logic in `src/components/TetEffects.tsx` will now check this state, ensuring no auto-play triggers, as I want the manual pause button to override any subsequent user interactions. I'll need to update the `toggleMusic` function too. The YouTube IFrame API is the tool in question.


**Refining Pause Intent**

I've refined my approach to managing manual pauses. I'm moving away from `hasManuallyPaused` and introducing `userWantsMusic` with a default of `true`. This new state will determine the user's intent to keep the music playing. The auto-play logic will only run if the user *hasn't* explicitly stopped the music and it's not currently playing. This aims to create a more intuitive user experience.


**Implementing Stop Functionality**

I'm now focusing on a new strategy to definitively stop music playback. If the user clicks the music toggle, `hasInteracted` becomes `true`. The "unlock" logic will now only run if `!hasInteracted`. This will make the user click the toggle to set `hasInteracted(true)` and the player will stop trying to "force" play based on global movements. This directly addresses the user's need for a consistent "Stop" state regardless of other interactions.


**Adding Manual Override**

I'm now incorporating a manual override to control music playback definitively. I've added `isStoppedManually` and `setIsStoppedManually` to my state management within `TetControls`. The `toggleMusic` function now explicitly sets `isStoppedManually` to `true` on pause and `false` on play. I've also modified the autoplay useEffect, adding a check to prevent playback if `isStoppedManually` is true. This implementation directly influences the `unlockAudio` behavior to prioritize the user's explicit stop action.


**Clarifying Interaction Priorities**

I'm now prioritizing the user's explicit interaction with the music button. I realize the `unlockAudio` logic could interfere immediately after a button press, which is undesired behavior. So, `unlockAudio` will be removed once the button is clicked, or the music successfully plays. More recently, I'm working to add a flag that will entirely disable auto-play logic when the user clicks "Stop". This will be implemented inside `src/components/TetEffects.tsx`.

entries.description: Update TetControls to handle manual stopping of music more robustly, ensuring that once the user pauses the music, it won't restart automatically due to other page interactions (scroll, touch, etc.).
